metadata.scheme_name: Reimbursement of Tuition Fee and Transport Facility to Scheduled Castes Students Studying in Technical Institutions in Haryana

raw_data.details: The scheme "Reimbursement of Tuition Fee and Transport Facility to Scheduled Castes Students Studying in Technical Institutions in Haryana" was launched by the Department of Technical Education, Government of Haryana. It aims to provide financial assistance to Scheduled Castes students pursuing AICTE-approved technical courses, such as Diplomas from Polytechnics, D-Pharmacy, B-Pharmacy, B. Tech., M. E., M. Tech., Ph. D. in Engineering subjects, MBA, and MCA. The scheme is applicable only to students enrolled in regular mode at government, private, or aided technical institutions approved by the Technical Education Department, Haryana. Under this scheme, eligible students receive reimbursement of tuition fees along with transport facility benefits.
raw_data.benefits: Tuition Fee Reimbursement:The reimbursement covers the actual tuition fee of ₹20,000 for diploma courses and ₹40,000 for degree courses, whichever is lower. Transport Facility Reimbursement:The reimbursement covers the actual amount of state transport facilities (train pass/bus pass).
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a Scheduled Caste student of Haryana. The student should be enrolled in an AICTE-approved Diploma, Undergraduate, or Postgraduate technical course, including Diploma from Polytechnics, D-Pharmacy, B-Pharmacy, B. Tech., M. E., M. Tech., Ph. D. in Engineering subjects, MBA, or MCA. The applicant should be pursuing an AICTE-approved course in a government, private, or aided technical institution approved by the Technical Education Department, Haryana. The applicant should be admitted through centralized counselling. The applicant’s annual parental income should be between ₹2. 5 lakh and ₹3. 5 lakh for tuition fee reimbursement. The applicant should not be residing in a campus hostel to be eligible for transport facility reimbursement.
raw_data.exclusions: The applicant should not be a resident of a campus hostel for transport facility reimbursement. The applicant should not be pursuing a technical course through distance education or part-time mode.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineRegistration Process:
Step 1: Click the Registration link at the[Portal](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/), if you are a new user. Existing users can click on the login button.
Step 2: Enter the Parivar Pehchaan Patra (Family Id), and a List of members will be displayed, Select your Name and Click on the "Generate OTP" button.
Step 3: Enter the OTP received on your registered Mobile Number and Click on the "Verify OTP" button.
Step 4: After OTP Verification, all the details get auto-filled in the Registration Form, cross-check your information.
Step 5: Select Your Department and College, create your Password, and click on the "Register" Button. Application Process:
Step 1: Click the login link at the [Portal](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/UserLogin) and enter your User ID and password to apply for the scholarship.
Step 2: Check eligibility for the scholarship and select the scholarship scheme.
Step 3: Click on the “Apply” button and provide all necessary details like Course, Roll No., Eligibility, etc.
Step 4: Save your application and then submit the application form after careful verification. Application Tracking:The students can check their [Scholarship Application](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/ApplicationStatus) Status using their details like their Aadhaar Number, Academic Session, and scheme applied for. Note: Applications are invited to the [Harchhatravratti portal](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in). Applications received after the due date will not be entertained. Detailed advertisement and scheme notifications are available under the "Notification" link on the [Department website](https://www.techeduhry.gov.in/).
raw_data.documents_required: Aadhaar Number/UID Number of the CandidateProof of Admission through Centralized CounselingIncome CertificateCaste CertificateFee Receipt from the InstitutionTransport Pass (Train/Bus Pass)Bank Account Details of the ApplicantAny other documents as required

Documents RequiredAadhaar Number/UID Number of the CandidateProof of Admission through Centralized CounselingIncome CertificateCaste CertificateFee Receipt from the InstitutionTransport Pass (Train/Bus Pass)Bank Account Details of the ApplicantAny other documents as requiredFrequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the objective of the Reimbursement of Tuition Fee and Transport Facility scheme? The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to Scheduled Castes students in Haryana pursuing AICTE-approved technical courses by reimbursing tuition fees and transport costs. Who is eligible for tuition fee reimbursement under this scheme? Scheduled Castes students of Haryana with an annual parental income between ₹2. 5 lakh and ₹3. 5 lakh, admitted through centralized counselling, are eligible for tuition fee reimbursement. What is the amount of tuition fee reimbursement provided? Eligible students can receive reimbursement of the actual tuition fee or ₹20,000 for diploma courses and ₹40,000 for degree courses, whichever is lower. What transport costs are covered under the scheme? The scheme reimburses the actual amount of state transport facilities, including train passes and bus passes. What is the application process for this scheme? Students must submit their applications on the Harchhatravratti portal (https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in) within the specified application period. What documents are required for application? Applicants need to provide Aadhaar/UID, proof of admission through centralized counselling, income certificate, fee receipt, transport pass (if applicable), and bank account details. Can students admitted through management quota apply? No, only students admitted through centralized counselling are eligible for tuition fee reimbursement. Are diploma students eligible for this scheme? Yes, diploma students studying AICTE-approved courses are eligible. Does this scheme apply to students pursuing Ph. D. courses? Yes, PhD students in engineering subjects are eligible under this scheme. Can students apply offline for this scheme? No, applications must be submitted online through the Harchhatravratti portal. Can students apply offline for this scheme? No, applications must be submitted online through the Harchhatravratti portal. Can students pursuing lateral entry courses apply? If admitted through centralized counselling in an AICTE-approved institution, they may be eligible. Where can students find official updates about this scheme? Official updates and notifications are available under the "Notification" link on www.techeduhry.nic.in. Sources And References[Guideline](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/Notices/Reimbursement%20of%20Tuition%20Fee%20and%20Transport%20Facility.pdf)OkWas this helpful?
raw_data.scheme_name: Reimbursement of Tuition Fee and Transport Facility to Scheduled Castes Students Studying in Technical Institutions in Haryana

faqs.question: What is the objective of the Reimbursement of Tuition Fee and Transport Facility scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to Scheduled Castes students in Haryana pursuing AICTE-approved technical courses by reimbursing tuition fees and transport costs.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for tuition fee reimbursement under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Scheduled Castes students of Haryana with an annual parental income between ₹2. 5 lakh and ₹3. 5 lakh, admitted through centralized counselling, are eligible for tuition fee reimbursement.

faqs.question: What is the amount of tuition fee reimbursement provided?
faqs.answer: Eligible students can receive reimbursement of the actual tuition fee or ₹20,000 for diploma courses and ₹40,000 for degree courses, whichever is lower.

faqs.question: What transport costs are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme reimburses the actual amount of state transport facilities, including train passes and bus passes.

faqs.question: What is the application process for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students must submit their applications on the Harchhatravratti portal (https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in) within the specified application period.

faqs.question: What documents are required for application?
faqs.answer: Applicants need to provide Aadhaar/UID, proof of admission through centralized counselling, income certificate, fee receipt, transport pass (if applicable), and bank account details.

faqs.question: Can students admitted through management quota apply?
faqs.answer: No, only students admitted through centralized counselling are eligible for tuition fee reimbursement.

faqs.question: Are diploma students eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, diploma students studying AICTE-approved courses are eligible.

faqs.question: Does this scheme apply to students pursuing Ph. D. courses?
faqs.answer: Yes, PhD students in engineering subjects are eligible under this scheme.

faqs.question: Can students apply offline for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, applications must be submitted online through the Harchhatravratti portal.

faqs.question: Can students pursuing lateral entry courses apply?
faqs.answer: If admitted through centralized counselling in an AICTE-approved institution, they may be eligible.

faqs.question: Where can students find official updates about this scheme?
faqs.answer: Official updates and notifications are available under the "Notification" link on www.techeduhry.nic.in.
